idea.hook: Our ENTIRE Solar System... is TINY! 🤯
facts: Earth feels huge, but 1.3 MILLION Earths fit inside our Sun!
Our Sun? A mere speck compared to UY Scuti, one of the largest known stars.
If UY Scuti replaced our Sun, its surface would extend BEYOND Jupiter's orbit (5.2 AU)!
idea.payoff: Our home planetary system would be completely SWALLOWED!
idea.title: Our Solar System Swallowed Whole! 🤯🌌

scenes.visual: A vast, dark space background with our Solar System (planets orbiting a central, small Sun) appearing as a distant, intricate system. Stars twinkle faintly.

scenes.text:
...is TINY! 🤯
1.3 MILLION Earths fit in our Sun!
scenes.visual: The camera quickly zooms towards the Sun, filling the screen. Earths rapidly shrink into tiny dots and get absorbed or represented by a counter showing them fitting inside the Sun's silhouette.

scenes.text: Our Sun? A mere speck compared to UY Scuti!
scenes.visual: The large Sun from the previous scene shrinks rapidly to a tiny speck in the center. From the left, an impossibly giant, red-orange star (UY Scuti) slides in, quickly dwarfing and then completely obscuring the Sun.

scenes.text: UY Scuti's surface extends BEYOND Jupiter's orbit!
scenes.visual: UY Scuti fills the entire screen with its turbulent surface visible. A translucent ring representing Jupiter's orbit (labeled 'Jupiter's Orbit') briefly appears from the right, then UY Scuti visually expands to clearly engulf and pass beyond this ring.

scenes.text: Our ENTIRE solar system would be SWALLOWED!
scenes.visual: UY Scuti's immense, fiery surface dominates the screen. Faint, ghost-like outlines of the inner planets (Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars) and Jupiter's orbit momentarily appear within its fiery expanse, emphasizing their complete engulfment before fading away. The star's surface pulses with a slight, ominous glow.
